description: Language-specific analysis prompts for Scrutin AI code review

securityFocus: SQL Injection via string concatenation in database queries (use parameterized queries or ORM)
Path traversal through unsanitized file path inputs (validate and sanitize paths)
Command injection via Process.Start with user input (avoid shell execution with user input)
XXE vulnerabilities in XML parsing (disable DTD: XmlReaderSettings.DtdProcessing = Prohibit)
XSS in ASP.NET applications (use encoding and HtmlSanitizer)
SSRF when making HTTP requests with user-supplied URLs (validate and whitelist domains)
Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or connection strings (use secrets management / IConfiguration)
Weak cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES) — use SHA256+ and AES
Insecure random number generation (use RandomNumberGenerator, not System.Random for security)
Weak SSL/TLS protocols (enforce TLS 1.2+, disable SSLv3/TLS 1.0)
bugPatterns: Null reference exceptions — missing null checks before member access
Async void methods that swallow exceptions (use async Task instead)
Infinite recursion without base case
Double dispose of IDisposable objects
Race conditions in multi-threaded code without proper locking
Unreachable code after return/throw statements
Collection modified during enumeration
Route parameter type mismatch — controller expects int/Guid but route template allows any string
Missing [FromBody]/[FromQuery]/[FromRoute] attributes causing incorrect model binding
Missing ModelState.IsValid check (or [ApiController] auto-validation not applied)
Missing CancellationToken propagation in async chains (tokens created but not passed downstream)
performancePatterns: HttpClient instantiated per-request causing connection exhaustion — use IHttpClientFactory or a static/singleton HttpClient
HttpClient without timeout — requests may hang indefinitely (set Timeout property or use Polly timeout policy)
DbContext not disposed or scoped incorrectly causing connection pool exhaustion
N+1 queries in Entity Framework (use Include/ThenInclude for eager loading)
Synchronous database calls in async context (use async versions — ToListAsync, FirstOrDefaultAsync)
String concatenation in loops (use StringBuilder)
Boxing/unboxing in hot paths
LINQ queries that enumerate multiple times (materialize with ToList/ToArray once)
Large object allocations in frequently called methods
Missing ConfigureAwait(false) in library code causing deadlocks in sync-over-async contexts
bestPractices: Use records for immutable DTOs
Prefer async/await over Task.Result or .Wait() (causes deadlocks in ASP.NET context)
Use ILogger instead of Console.WriteLine
Follow repository pattern for data access
Use dependency injection instead of new keyword for services
Avoid public fields, use properties
Controllers should delegate to services (thin controllers)

aspnetcore: Missing CSRF protection on state-changing operations
Exposing stack traces in production (disable detailed errors in appsettings.Production.json)
Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoints
Insecure CORS configuration (avoid wildcard origins with credentials)
entityframework: Missing transaction for multiple related operations
Tracking queries when read-only would suffice (use AsNoTracking())
Missing index hints for frequently filtered columns
Open DbContext longer than a single unit of work

securityFocus: XSS via innerHTML or React dangerous HTML props (use textContent or sanitize with DOMPurify)
Prototype pollution through Object.assign or lodash merge with user input
Hard-coded secrets, API keys, or tokens (use environment variables via process.env)
Dynamic code execution risks (avoid eval and new Function constructor)
Permissive CORS configuration in APIs
Insecure localStorage usage for sensitive data (use httpOnly cookies)
URL manipulation without validation (validate URLs before use)
JWT secrets hard-coded or weak (use strong random secrets, rotate regularly)
bugPatterns: Missing await on async functions (fire-and-forget — errors silently lost)
Ignored Promise rejections (always handle with .catch() or try/catch)
Type assertions (as) bypassing actual type safety
Optional chaining without fallback for required values
Array/object mutation when immutability expected
Strict equality issues (=== vs ==)
Wrong HTTP route path — typo or missing leading slash in API route definition
Missing request body/query parameter validation (use zod, joi, or class-validator)
Type mismatch in API handler — expecting number but JSON sends string (use parseInt/coercion)
fetch/axios without timeout — requests may hang indefinitely (use AbortController with setTimeout)
performancePatterns: Creating new database connection/client per request instead of using a shared connection pool
HTTP client (axios/fetch) not reused — configure a shared instance with base URL and interceptors
Unnecessary re-renders in React (missing memo, useMemo, useCallback for expensive operations)
Large bundle imports (import specific modules instead of entire libraries)
Synchronous operations blocking the event loop
Memory leaks from uncleared intervals/timeouts/subscriptions (cleanup in useEffect return)
bestPractices: Use strict TypeScript configuration (strict: true in tsconfig.json)
Prefer unknown over any type
Use const assertions for literal types
Avoid type assertions, prefer type guards
Use interface for object shapes, type for unions/intersections
Handle all Promise rejections — attach .catch() or use unhandledRejection handler

react: Missing key prop in list rendering (use stable unique keys, not array index)
Missing or incorrect useEffect dependencies (causes stale closures or infinite loops)
State updates on unmounted components (cleanup in useEffect)
Mutating state directly instead of using setState
nextjs: Exposing server secrets to client (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix leaks to browser bundle)
Missing error boundaries for client components
Improper use of 'use client' directive — pushing too much to client bundle

securityFocus: XSS through DOM manipulation with user input (innerHTML, document.write)
Dynamic code execution risks (avoid eval and new Function constructor)
Hard-coded credentials or API keys
Prototype pollution vulnerabilities (Object.assign, merge functions with user input)
Insecure regular expressions (ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking)
Open redirects with unvalidated URLs
bugPatterns: Implicit global variables (missing var/let/const)
Callback hell without error handling
Type coercion issues (use strict equality ===)
Empty catch blocks swallowing errors silently
This binding issues in callbacks (use arrow functions or .bind())
Hoisting confusion with var
fetch/axios without timeout or AbortController — connection may hang indefinitely
Missing input type validation before using as number, date, or object key
performancePatterns: Creating new DB connection per request instead of reusing a connection pool
Synchronous file/IO operations in Node.js blocking the event loop (use async variants)
Memory leaks from closures retaining large objects or event listeners not removed
Excessive DOM manipulation (batch updates, use DocumentFragment)
Blocking operations in main thread
bestPractices: Use const by default, let when reassignment needed, avoid var
Use template literals instead of string concatenation
Use arrow functions for callbacks
Use destructuring for cleaner code
Handle all Promise rejections (.catch() or try/catch in async)
Enable strict mode ('use strict')

securityFocus: SQL injection through string formatting in queries (use parameterized queries with %s or ?)
Command injection via subprocess with shell=True (use subprocess.run with a list, never shell=True with user input)
Pickle deserialization of untrusted data (use JSON instead)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
SSL certificate verification disabled (verify=False in requests)
YAML unsafe load (use yaml.safe_load instead of yaml.load)
Path traversal in file operations
bugPatterns: Mutable default arguments in functions (def f(x=[]) — shared across all calls)
Bare except clauses catching all exceptions including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt
Variable shadowing in nested scopes
Late binding closures in loops (lambdas capturing loop variable by reference)
requests library used without timeout parameter — connection may hang indefinitely (add timeout=30)
Database connections not closed or not returned to pool (use context managers or connection pooling)
performancePatterns: String concatenation in loops (use ''.join(list) instead)
Repeated list/dict lookups in hot path (cache in local variable)
Not using generators for large datasets (loading everything into memory at once)
Opening too many files or DB connections without closing them (use 'with' statement)
Global Interpreter Lock bottlenecks — use multiprocessing for CPU-bound tasks
bestPractices: Use type hints (typing module) for all function signatures
Follow PEP 8 style guide
Use context managers (with statement) for all resources — files, DB connections, locks
Prefer list comprehensions over map/filter for readability
Use f-strings for string formatting (Python 3.6+)
Use pathlib instead of os.path for file operations

securityFocus: SQL injection through string concatenation (use db.Query with placeholders $1 or ?)
Command injection via exec.Command with shell-interpreted user input
Hard-coded credentials or secrets in source code
Insecure TLS configuration (InsecureSkipVerify: true — never in production)
Path traversal in file handling (sanitize with filepath.Clean and validate prefix)
Race conditions in goroutines accessing shared state without mutex
bugPatterns: Ignoring error return values (all errors in Go must be checked)
Goroutine leaks — goroutines started without a way to stop or cancel them
Nil pointer dereference without nil check before access
Data races — shared state accessed from multiple goroutines without sync.Mutex or atomic
Deferred function call inside a loop causing resource exhaustion (defer runs at function end, not loop end)
Integer overflow in calculations
sql.Rows not closed after query — causes DB connection pool exhaustion (use defer rows.Close())
http.Response.Body not closed after reading — causes TCP connection leak (use defer resp.Body.Close())
Context without timeout for external calls — HTTP or DB requests may hang indefinitely
performancePatterns: http.Client created per-request — reuse a shared http.Client with transport configured (connection pooling built-in)
http.Client without Timeout field — requests may hang indefinitely
DB connection pool not configured (db.SetMaxOpenConns, db.SetMaxIdleConns, db.SetConnMaxLifetime)
Excessive allocations in hot paths (use sync.Pool for frequently allocated objects)
Inefficient string building using + in loops (use strings.Builder)
Unbuffered channels causing blocking when buffer would suffice
Excessive goroutine creation — use worker pool pattern with semaphore or errgroup
bestPractices: Always handle errors explicitly — do not use _ for error return values
Use defer for cleanup of resources (files, DB rows, response bodies)
Prefer composition over inheritance (embed structs)
Use context for cancellation and deadlines across goroutine boundaries
Keep functions small and focused
Use interfaces for dependency injection and testability

securityFocus: SQL injection through string concatenation (use PreparedStatement with ?)
XXE vulnerabilities in XML parsing (disable DOCTYPE, set FEATURE_SECURE_PROCESSING)
Deserialization of untrusted data via ObjectInputStream
LDAP injection in directory queries
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
Path traversal in file operations
Weak cryptography (MD5, SHA1, DES) — use SHA-256+ and AES-256
bugPatterns: Null pointer exceptions (missing null checks before member access)
Resource leaks — streams, connections, ResultSet not closed (use try-with-resources)
Equals and hashCode contract violations
ConcurrentModificationException — collection modified during iteration
Infinite loops without exit condition
Missing @Valid/@Validated on controller method parameters (request body not validated)
Route mapping returns wrong HTTP status for error cases (returning 200 on validation error)
performancePatterns: HttpClient instances not reused — create once (static/singleton), reuse across requests
JDBC Connection not closed or not returned to pool — use try-with-resources or connection pool
Connection pool misconfigured — too few connections (HikariCP maxPoolSize too low for load)
Missing transaction rollback on exception — partial writes left in inconsistent state
String concatenation in loops (use StringBuilder)
Boxing/unboxing in loops (use primitive types)
N+1 query problems with JPA/Hibernate (use JOIN FETCH or @BatchSize)
Inefficient collection operations
bestPractices: Use try-with-resources for AutoCloseable (Connection, Stream, ResultSet)
Prefer Optional<T> over null returns from methods
Use final for immutable fields
Follow SOLID principles
Use dependency injection (Spring @Autowired, @Bean)
Write unit tests for all public methods

securityFocus: SQL injection in database queries (use parameterized queries)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
Insecure network configuration (cleartext traffic, disabled certificate validation)
Path traversal vulnerabilities
bugPatterns: Null safety bypasses (!! operator overuse — use safe call ?. or let {})
Coroutine scope leaks (GlobalScope usage, coroutines not cancelled on lifecycle end)
Late init var used before initialization
Type casting without safety (as instead of as? — throws ClassCastException)
Missing timeout in coroutine network or DB calls (use withTimeout {})
performancePatterns: Creating new OkHttpClient per request — reuse a shared instance with connection pool
Blocking calls on Dispatchers.Main (use Dispatchers.IO for all IO operations)
Unnecessary object allocations in hot paths
Inefficient collection operations (prefer sequences for large collections)
Database connections not returned to pool
bestPractices: Use data classes for DTOs
Prefer val over var
Use sealed classes for restricted hierarchies (state machines, result types)
Use extension functions appropriately — avoid polluting types
Handle nullability properly with safe calls and elvis operator

securityFocus: SQL injection through string interpolation in ActiveRecord queries (use ? placeholders or Arel)
Command injection via backticks, system(), or Open3 with user input
Mass assignment vulnerabilities in Rails (use strong parameters — permit only expected attrs)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
Unsafe YAML loading (Psych.safe_load instead of YAML.load)
CSRF protection bypass (protect_from_forgery should not be disabled)
bugPatterns: Method visibility issues (public methods intended to be private)
Mutable default values in method signatures
Exception handling too broad (rescue StandardError vs rescue specific exception)
Missing HTTP timeout on external calls (Net::HTTP, Faraday, HTTParty without timeout)
performancePatterns: N+1 queries in ActiveRecord (use includes, eager_load, or preload)
Database connections not returned to pool (ActiveRecord connection pool exhaustion)
Inefficient database queries (select * when only few columns needed)
Memory bloat from loading large ActiveRecord collections (use find_each with batches)
bestPractices: Follow Ruby style guide (enforce with RuboCop)
Use strong parameters in Rails controllers
Prefer symbols over strings for hash keys
Use service objects for complex business logic

securityFocus: SQL injection through string concatenation (use PDO prepared statements with ? or :param)
XSS through unescaped output (use htmlspecialchars() or Twig auto-escape)
Command injection via shell execution functions (shell_exec, passthru, system with user input)
File inclusion vulnerabilities (require/include with user-controlled path)
Hard-coded credentials in source code
Insecure session configuration (session.cookie_httponly, session.cookie_secure not set)
Unrestricted file upload (missing MIME type validation and extension whitelist)
bugPatterns: Type coercion issues — loose comparison (== instead of ===) causing unexpected matches
Undefined variable usage without isset() check
Reference vs value confusion in arrays (& modifier side effects)
HTTP requests without timeout — file_get_contents or cURL without CURLOPT_TIMEOUT
performancePatterns: N+1 queries in database operations (eager load with JOINs or ORM relationships)
Creating new database connection per request instead of using PDO persistent connections
Large file processing without streaming (use fgets/fread instead of file_get_contents for large files)
bestPractices: Use strict type declarations (declare(strict_types=1))
Follow PSR-12 coding standards
Use dependency injection and containers
Validate all input data with filter_var or a validation library

securityFocus: Unsafe blocks with memory safety issues — document invariants and minimize scope
SQL injection in raw queries (use sqlx bind parameters, not format! macro)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
Integer overflow — use checked_add/checked_mul or saturating arithmetic in critical paths
bugPatterns: Panic in production code via unwrap() or expect() on None/Err (use ? operator or match)
Deadlocks from incorrect lock ordering (always acquire multiple locks in the same order)
Memory leaks through Rc<RefCell<T>> reference cycles (use Weak for back-references)
HTTP client (reqwest) created per-request — build Client once and reuse (connection pooling built-in)
performancePatterns: Unnecessary cloning of large data structures (pass references where ownership not needed)
Blocking operations inside async context (use tokio::task::spawn_blocking for CPU-bound work)
Inefficient string operations — use String::with_capacity when size is known
Excessive heap allocations in hot paths (use stack-allocated arrays when size is bounded)
bestPractices: Use clippy and address all warnings before merging
Prefer ? operator over unwrap/expect in functions that return Result
Use appropriate error types (thiserror for libraries, anyhow for applications)
Document public APIs with doc comments including examples
Write tests including edge cases and error paths

securityFocus: Buffer overflows — use strncpy, snprintf; avoid strcpy/sprintf; always check bounds
Format string vulnerabilities — always use format specifiers in printf/scanf
Use after free and double free — set pointers to nullptr after freeing
Integer overflows in arithmetic — check bounds before operations, use safe integer libraries
NULL pointer dereferences — validate pointers before dereferencing
Uninitialized variable usage — always initialize variables at declaration
Memory leaks — every malloc/calloc must have a corresponding free (prefer RAII/smart pointers)
Unsafe string functions — avoid gets(); use fgets() with size limit
Command injection via system() or popen() with user-controlled input — sanitize all input
TOCTOU race conditions in file operations
Return of stack address — never return pointers to local variables
Hard-coded credentials or encryption keys
bugPatterns: Missing break in switch statements causing fall-through
Off-by-one errors in array indexing and loops
Signed/unsigned comparison issues causing incorrect branch logic
Resource leaks — files not closed, sockets not closed, memory not freed on error paths
Dangling pointers after freeing memory
Incorrect use of sizeof (sizeof(ptr) vs sizeof(*ptr))
Infinite loops without proper termination conditions
Stack overflow from deep recursion or large stack allocations
Incorrect error handling — ignoring return values of system calls (open, read, write)
File descriptor or socket exhaustion — not closing on all error paths
performancePatterns: Unnecessary memory copies (use pointers or references instead of passing large objects by value)
Inefficient string concatenation in loops
Repeated calculations that could be cached in a local variable
Poor cache locality in data structures
Unnecessary dynamic allocations in hot paths (prefer stack or pool allocation)
Virtual function calls in performance-critical inner loops
Not using const references for large object parameters
bestPractices: Use RAII for all resource management (files, memory, locks, sockets)
Prefer smart pointers (unique_ptr, shared_ptr) over raw pointers
Use const correctness for function parameters and methods
Initialize all variables at declaration
Check return values of all system calls and allocation functions
Use static analysis tools (cppcheck, clang-static-analyzer, AddressSanitizer)
Prefer std::vector over raw arrays
Use nullptr instead of NULL or 0 for null pointers

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or tokens in source code (use Keychain or environment config)
Insecure URLSession configuration — disabled certificate validation
Sensitive data logged via print() — avoid logging PII, passwords, or tokens
Keychain misuse — storing secrets in UserDefaults instead of Keychain
Cleartext HTTP traffic (missing NSAllowsArbitraryLoads restriction in Info.plist)
bugPatterns: Force unwrapping with ! on Optional — use guard let, if let, or ?? instead
Force casting with as! — use as? and handle nil case explicitly
Retain cycles in closures — capture self weakly ([weak self]) in escaping closures
URLSession data tasks without timeout — requests may hang indefinitely
NotificationCenter observer not removed on deinit — causes crashes on deallocated observer
Accessing UIKit/SwiftUI from background thread — all UI updates must be on main thread
performancePatterns: URLSession shared instance overused — configure a custom session with timeout and cache policy
Blocking main thread with synchronous network calls (use async/await or completion handlers)
Excessive view re-rendering due to unnecessary @State/@ObservedObject updates
Large images loaded without resizing/downsampling before display
Memory leaks from strong reference cycles in delegate patterns
bestPractices: Use guard let/if let for safe Optional unwrapping
Use Result<Success, Failure> type for error-returning functions
Prefer value types (struct, enum) over reference types (class) where appropriate
Use weak/unowned references to break retain cycles
Mark classes as final when subclassing is not intended

securityFocus: SQL injection in raw query strings (use parameterized queries with Slick/Doobie bind variables)
Hard-coded credentials or secrets in source code
Unsafe deserialization of untrusted data
Missing input validation on HTTP request parameters and body
bugPatterns: Blocking a Future with Await.result in a thread pool context — causes thread starvation
Using null instead of Option[T] — prefer Option and pattern matching
Mutable shared state without synchronization (use Atomic or Actor model)
Uncaught exceptions in Future — always attach .recover or .recoverWith
HTTP client created per request — reuse a shared client (Akka HTTP, sttp) with connection pool
performancePatterns: Blocking IO operations on Futures running on computation ExecutionContext (use IO ExecutionContext)
Collecting entire DB resultset into memory instead of streaming (use Slick streaming/Reactive Streams)
Unnecessary serialization/deserialization in hot paths
Excessive object creation triggering GC pressure
bestPractices: Prefer Option[T] over null; use Either[Error, T] for error handling in functions
Use sealed traits for ADTs to enable exhaustive pattern matching
Handle all Future failures — a Future with no failure handling loses errors silently
Use case classes for immutable data
Avoid mutable state; prefer immutable collections

securityFocus: SOQL injection — never concatenate user input into dynamic SOQL (use Database.query with bind variables)
XSS in Visualforce controllers — escape output with HTMLENCODE()
Hard-coded Salesforce IDs or credentials (use Custom Metadata / Custom Settings)
Missing 'with sharing' keyword — data visibility rules not enforced
Insecure direct object reference — missing ownership check before DML on records
bugPatterns: SOQL queries inside for loops — violates governor limits (max 100 SOQL per transaction)
DML operations inside for loops — violates governor limits (max 150 DML per transaction)
Hardcoded record type IDs or user IDs — not portable between orgs
Trigger without handler class — logic directly in trigger body is not testable
Missing null check on relationship fields accessed via dot notation
performancePatterns: Non-bulkified triggers — code must handle collections of records, not single records
Using Database.query when a static SOQL query would suffice (less efficient)
Querying more fields than needed in SOQL
Not using SOQL aggregate queries (COUNT, SUM) when only the aggregate value is needed
bestPractices: Always use 'with sharing' unless 'without sharing' is explicitly required and documented
Write bulk-safe code — all DML and SOQL must operate on collections
Use trigger handler pattern (one trigger per object, delegate to handler class)
Achieve 100% test coverage with meaningful assertions
Use Custom Metadata Types for configuration instead of hard-coded values

securityFocus: Format string vulnerabilities via Printf with user-controlled format string
Hard-coded credentials or secrets
Insufficient input validation before processing external data
bugPatterns: Non-exhaustive pattern matching — missing cases cause Match_failure at runtime
Integer overflow in arithmetic operations
Ignored exceptions from functions that return unit — use try/with
Mutable state shared across threads without synchronization (use Mutex)
performancePatterns: Non-tail-recursive functions on large lists — causes stack overflow (use tail-recursive variants with accumulator)
Excessive allocations from list concatenation with @ (use List.rev_append or Buffer for strings)
Repeated list traversals that could be combined into one pass
Lazy values forced multiple times when result should be cached
bestPractices: Handle all match cases explicitly — use _ wildcard only when truly intentional
Use Result or option types for error handling instead of exceptions for control flow
Prefer immutable data structures
Use the type system to enforce invariants at compile time

securityFocus: XSS via unescaped EL expressions (${user.name} — use fn:escapeXml or c:out)
SQL injection in scriptlets that build query strings by concatenation
Session fixation — not invalidating session on authentication
Sensitive data exposed in HTML comments (visible in page source)
bugPatterns: Missing null checks on EL expressions — NullPointerException at render time
Unclosed resources (Connection, Statement, ResultSet) in scriptlets
Business logic in JSP scriptlets instead of Servlet/Controller layer (violates MVC)
performancePatterns: Heavy business logic or database queries directly in JSP (move to Servlet/Service layer)
Not using JSTL sql tags with connection pooling
Synchronizing on JSP page object — serializes all requests to the page
bestPractices: Use JSTL (c:out, c:forEach) instead of scriptlets
Use c:out or fn:escapeXml for all user-provided output to prevent XSS
Follow MVC pattern — JSP only for presentation, no business logic
Use JSP include directive for reusable fragments

securityFocus: XSS via apex:outputText or apex:outputLabel with escape='false' (default is true — do not override)
Exposing sensitive record fields without checking user permissions in controller
SOQL injection in controller methods backing VF components (use bind variables)
bugPatterns: Controller action methods not checking record ownership before DML
Missing null check on controller properties accessed in VF expressions
Large data sets rendered without pagination — causes view state and memory issues
View state too large — store large data in transient variables
performancePatterns: Fetching all records without LIMIT in controller SOQL (use pagination / StandardSetController)
Non-bulkified controller actions performing DML inside loops
Expensive operations in getter methods (called multiple times per page render — cache result)
bestPractices: Always use escape='true' on outputText (it is the default — do not override to false)
Use 'with sharing' on all Apex controllers
Use StandardSetController for list views with pagination
Avoid inline SOQL in getters — query once in constructor or init action

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials, passwords, or secrets in .tf or .tfvars files (use Vault, environment variables, or cloud secret manager data sources)
Overly permissive IAM policies — wildcard actions (*) or wildcard resources (*) in production
Public network access to databases or storage — ensure databases are not publicly accessible
Unencrypted storage — S3 buckets, RDS, EBS volumes should have encryption enabled
S3 bucket without block_public_access settings — can expose data publicly
Security group with wide port ranges open to 0.0.0.0/0 — overly permissive ingress
Missing deletion protection on production databases (deletion_protection = false)
Sensitive output values exposed without sensitive = true flag
bugPatterns: Provider version not pinned — required_providers version constraint missing, breaks on provider update
Missing required_providers block — implicit provider version causes inconsistency across environments
Missing depends_on for implicit resource dependencies not captured by references
count used instead of for_each for resources with keys — causes ordering issues on removal
Referencing data source that may not exist without a lifecycle precondition
Missing backend configuration — state stored locally is not suitable for team use
performancePatterns: Data source queried on every plan when result is stable — use locals or outputs to cache
Large number of resources in a single state file — split into modules/workspaces to reduce blast radius
Missing lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true } for resources that cause downtime on replacement
bestPractices: Use remote state backend with state locking (S3 + DynamoDB, Terraform Cloud, GitLab-managed)
Pin all provider versions with ~> X.Y
Use workspaces or separate state files for environments (dev/staging/prod)
Run terraform fmt and terraform validate in CI pipeline
Use tflint and tfsec/checkov for security scanning
Mark sensitive variables with sensitive = true to prevent leakage in plan output
Use modules for reusable infrastructure patterns

securityFocus: Running as root — always add USER instruction with a non-root user before CMD/ENTRYPOINT
Secrets in ENV or ARG instructions — visible in image layers and docker history (use build secrets or runtime injection)
Using ADD with remote URLs instead of COPY + RUN curl with checksum verification
Exposing unnecessary ports in EXPOSE — only expose what is needed
Installing packages without version pinning — not reproducible (pin versions: apt-get install curl=7.x)
bugPatterns: Using :latest tag for base image — not reproducible, can silently break builds (use specific version or digest)
Running apt-get update in a separate RUN from apt-get install — stale package index in cached layer
COPY . . before installing dependencies — invalidates dependency cache on every code change
Missing .dockerignore file — copies .git, node_modules, secrets into image
CMD as shell string instead of JSON array — PID 1 is shell, not app (signals not forwarded properly)
performancePatterns: Not using multi-stage builds — final image includes build tools, compilers, dev dependencies
Missing --no-install-recommends on apt-get install — installs unnecessary packages
Copying large directories unnecessarily before filtering (rely on .dockerignore)
Not combining RUN commands — creates unnecessary layers increasing image size
bestPractices: Use specific base image versions with digest pinning (FROM node:20.11.0-alpine)
Add HEALTHCHECK instruction for service containers
Run as non-root user (addgroup + adduser or useradd, then USER instruction)
Combine apt-get update and install in single RUN and clean up apt lists afterward
Use multi-stage builds to minimize final image size
Order instructions from least to most frequently changed to maximize layer cache hits

securityFocus: Privileged containers (privileged: true) — grants all Linux capabilities to container
Port bindings to all interfaces (- '5432:5432' exposes to network — use '127.0.0.1:5432:5432' for local-only services)
Secrets as plain-text environment variables — use Docker secrets or external secret manager
Missing read-only filesystem (read_only: true) for containers that do not need to write to disk
Mounting Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) — allows container to escape to host
bugPatterns: Missing depends_on with condition: service_healthy — service starts before dependency is ready
Hard-coded volume host paths — not portable across environments (use named volumes)
Missing restart policy — containers do not recover from crashes (add restart: unless-stopped)
Services sharing a network with more access than needed — use separate networks per service group
performancePatterns: Missing resource limits (deploy.resources.limits for memory and CPU) — single container can starve host
No health check defined for depends_on — compose cannot wait for readiness properly
Logging driver not configured — logs accumulate unbounded on host filesystem
bestPractices: Use named volumes instead of bind mounts for persistent data
Use .env file for environment-specific configuration (never commit .env with secrets)
Define resource limits for all services in production-like environments
Use healthcheck for all stateful services (databases, caches)
Use specific image versions, not :latest

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials or tokens in script steps — use CI/CD masked variables
Pulling and running unverified external scripts (curl https://... | bash) — verify checksums or use internal mirrors
Missing secret masking — variables containing secrets must be marked as Masked in CI/CD settings
Overly permissive job rules (when: always on protected branches) — require manual approval for production deployments
Using shell executor with insufficient isolation — prefer Docker executor
bugPatterns: Missing set -e in script blocks — errors silently ignored and job reports success
Cache key not including lock file — stale dependencies served from cache after package updates
Artifact paths do not match actual output paths — artifacts silently empty
Wrong stage ordering causing jobs to run before their dependencies complete
Missing needs or dependencies declaration — jobs wait unnecessarily in sequential stages
performancePatterns: Downloading dependencies on every job without cache — use cache: with correct key and paths
Not parallelizing independent jobs (test matrix, linting, security scan can run in parallel)
Uploading large artifacts that are not consumed by downstream jobs
Not using extends or !reference for shared job templates — duplicated configuration
bestPractices: Pin image tags with digest for reproducibility
Use extends or !reference for reusable job templates
Set timeout on jobs to prevent hung jobs from blocking runners indefinitely
Use needs: [] for DAG pipeline to skip unnecessary stage waits
Use environments with protection rules for production deployments
Run SAST and secret detection jobs on all merge requests

securityFocus: Hard-coded secrets in script steps — use secret pipeline variables or Azure Key Vault task
Missing secret masking — all sensitive variables must be marked as Secret in pipeline settings
Overly permissive service connections — scope to specific resource groups/subscriptions
Missing environment approvals for production deployments — add approval gates on environments
Using self-hosted agents for untrusted fork PR builds — fork PRs can access agent environment
bugPatterns: Missing dependsOn for pipeline stages — stages may run in wrong order
Condition expression referencing undefined variable — evaluates to empty string silently
Incorrect artifact download path — DownloadPipelineArtifact without explicit path causes confusion
continueOnError: true on critical steps — failures silently ignored and pipeline reports success
Missing checkout: self step in jobs that need source code
performancePatterns: Not caching dependencies between pipeline runs (NuGet, npm, pip cache tasks available)
Downloading unnecessary artifacts in every job — scope artifact downloads to jobs that need them
Sequential stages for independent workloads — use parallel jobs within a stage
bestPractices: Use pipeline templates (extends, template references) for reusable steps and policies
Pin task versions (e.g., DotNetCoreCLI@2) to avoid unexpected behavior after task updates
Use variable groups for shared configuration across pipelines
Store secrets in Azure Key Vault and reference via variable group + Key Vault link
Add timeout to jobs and steps to prevent indefinitely hanging pipelines

securityFocus: Hard-coded credentials, API keys, or connection strings in JSON config files
Sensitive default values in example/template JSON files committed to repository
bugPatterns: Trailing commas in JSON (not valid per spec — causes parse failure in strict parsers)
Incorrect data types for configuration values (string '80' instead of number 80 for port)
bestPractices: Use JSON Schema validation for configuration files
Keep JSON config files free of secrets — use environment-specific overrides outside the repo

securityFocus: XXE (XML External Entity) injection — DOCTYPE declarations with SYSTEM or PUBLIC entities
Sensitive data or credentials embedded in XML configuration files
XPath injection when building XPath queries from user input
bugPatterns: Missing XML namespace declarations causing parser failures
Encoding declaration mismatch between XML header and actual file encoding
performancePatterns: Loading entire large XML document into DOM (use SAX or StAX streaming parser for large files)
bestPractices: Disable DOCTYPE/DTD processing in XML parsers to prevent XXE
Use XML Schema (XSD) validation for configuration and data exchange files

securityFocus: Command injection via unquoted variables (always double-quote variable expansions: "$var")
Path traversal through unsanitized file paths
Unsafe evaluation of dynamic content (avoid eval on any external or user-controlled input)
Hard-coded credentials and API keys in scripts
Improper temporary file creation (use mktemp, never predictable names in /tmp)
Unsafe pipe-to-shell patterns — verify checksums or use package managers
Word splitting on unquoted variables causing unexpected behavior
Insecure use of sudo without input validation
bugPatterns: Missing error handling — use set -euo pipefail at script start
Uninitialized variables causing silent empty string substitution (use set -u or default: ${var:-default})
Incorrect test syntax ([ ] vs [[ ]] in Bash) — prefer [[ ]] for string comparisons
Missing shebang line — interpreter undefined
cd without error handling — subsequent commands run in wrong directory if cd fails
Using $? after multiple commands — $? only captures last command exit code
Missing quotes around variables with spaces causing word splitting and globbing
performancePatterns: Unnecessary subshells and command substitutions in tight loops
Useless use of cat (cat file | grep — use grep directly with file argument)
Multiple grep/sed/awk in a pipeline when single awk command would suffice
Forking external commands in tight loops (use Bash built-ins where possible)
bestPractices: Always use shebang (#!/usr/bin/env bash or #!/bin/sh for portability)
Enable strict mode: set -euo pipefail
Quote all variable expansions: "$var", "$@", "${array[@]}"
Use local for function-scoped variables
Prefer [[ ]] over [ ] for conditionals in Bash
Use $() instead of backticks for command substitution
Validate all external inputs before use
Use ShellCheck for static analysis

sqlInjection.description: Patterns to detect SQL injection vulnerabilities

commandInjection.description: Patterns to detect command injection vulnerabilities

resourceExhaustion.description: Patterns to detect HTTP/DB/file descriptor connection exhaustion

inputValidation.description: Patterns to detect missing input/parameter validation in API routes
